ideas.title: ⌘K command palette for cross-post + factory navigation (fuzzy search, no shell)
ideas.why: Signal: 399 commits/7d of content with no fast navigation; a Linear/Vercel-style palette expresses the 'CLI ergonomics' and 'Agent Experience (AX)' concepts and is the explicitly-allowed non-terminal power-nav that makes the site feel like a product.

ideas.title: Mutation-testing gate (cargo-mutants) on blog-logic to prove the 100% coverage isn't vacuous
ideas.why: Signal: coverage_pct=100 over only 7 fns plus a real self-heal revert show line-coverage can pass while assertions are weak; adding the ontology 'Mutation Testing' concept as a CI check narrows the 'Generation-verification gap' by killing surviving mutants before ship.

ideas.title: WebGL 3D force-directed knowledge graph of the 603-concept ontology
ideas.why: Signal: the brain holds 603 concepts across 7 domains with real links but shows them flat; a navigable 3D graph (borrowing Obsidian's graph view) makes the ontology's 'Software cartography', 'Graph community detection', and 'Bidirectional linking' concepts the site's memorable, shareable centerpiece.

ideas.title: In-browser open-weights tiny model (WASM) that generates the brain's live text client-side
ideas.why: World signal: open-weights releases, local-CPU inference, and heavyweight WASM ports dominate today's HN front page; compiling a tiny model to run entirely in the visitor's browser realizes the 'Foundation Model', 'TinyML / embedded inference', and 'Memory-mapped weights' concepts with zero backend — the grounded whoa-piece for this exact moment.

ideas.title: Related-posts engine (tag/keyword overlap) in blog-logic
ideas.why: Signal: 100% coverage gate + code_commits_7d=399 mean lots of posts but no discovery path; realizes the ontology concepts 'Bidirectional linking' and 'Entity resolution' as a real feature, borrowing Gwern/Notion's related-links pattern to make the site a place people keep reading.

ideas.title: RSS/Atom feed generated by a covered blog-logic serializer
ideas.why: Signal: audience-growth gap — the pipeline posts to LinkedIn but has no subscribe surface; grounds the 'Build in public' and 'Monotonic Knowledge Growth' concepts and matches every serious engineering blog (e.g. Julia Evans) that offers a feed.
